message:
Running command: 
echo "=== Draining the OpenROAD→LibreLane pipe ==="
# OpenROAD (fd 1,2) → pipe:3207830 → LibreLane (fd 11)
# Open the read end via /proc/956480/fd/11 and consume data
# This should unblock OpenROAD

# First, check how much data is in the pipe
echo "Pipe info before drain:"
cat /proc/956540/fdinfo/1 2>/dev/null  # write end info

# Drain the pipe with a timeout - read up to 128KB and save to file
timeout 10 head -c 131072 /proc/956480/fd/11 > /tmp/pipe_drain.txt 2>/dev/null
echo "Drained bytes: $(wc -c < /tmp/pipe_drain.txt)"
echo "Drained content:"
cat /tmp/pipe_drain.txt

echo ""
echo "=== CPU time after drain ==="
sleep 2
T1=$(ps -p 956540 -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
sleep 5
T2=$(ps -p 956540 -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
echo "T1=$T1 T2=$T2"
echo "OpenROAD syscall state:"
cat /proc/956540/syscall 2>/dev/null

function.name: execute_bash

args.command:
echo "=== Draining the OpenROAD→LibreLane pipe ==="
# OpenROAD (fd 1,2) → pipe:3207830 → LibreLane (fd 11)
# Open the read end via /proc/956480/fd/11 and consume data
# This should unblock OpenROAD

# First, check how much data is in the pipe
echo "Pipe info before drain:"
cat /proc/956540/fdinfo/1 2>/dev/null  # write end info

# Drain the pipe with a timeout - read up to 128KB and save to file
timeout 10 head -c 131072 /proc/956480/fd/11 > /tmp/pipe_drain.txt 2>/dev/null

echo "Drained content:"
